steps.title: Identify the key warning
steps.content: The passage cautions that small sample sizes can “contribute to reports of exaggerated effects.” Pimentel's study used a sample of only 12 fish.

steps.title: Match the warning to the choices
steps.content:
C directly matches the passage's warning: the conclusion “may overstate the effect,” which is what happens with small-sample exaggeration.

A and B make unsupported claims about the study; D overclaims by calling it “invalidated” when only potential bias is flagged.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
